events.notes: Genesis block: ~1.32B STX distributed at mainnet launch (Oct 2019) covering public sale, SAFT investors, foundation, and team allocations per the Blockstack token offering.
Team/employee allocation at genesis, subject to 3-year vesting with 1-year cliff beginning from mainnet launch (Oct 2019).
Blockstack PBC / Stacks Foundation treasury allocation at genesis for ecosystem development and grants.
Estimated first cliff unlock for team/employee tokens after 1-year cliff from Oct 2019 mainnet; remaining vest monthly through Oct 2022.
SAFT (2017) investor lockup fully expired approximately 2 years post-mainnet; exact tranche dates varied by investor agreement.
Final team vesting tranche completes ~3 years post-mainnet (Oct 2022); all early team lockups considered fully vested after this date.
Remaining ~882M STX emitted gradually as mining rewards anchored to Bitcoin blocks over ~30 years until ~2050 hard cap of ~1.818B total STX is reached.

summary: Stacks (STX) launched with a genesis supply of ~1.32B tokens in October 2019, with team and investor lockups that fully vested by approximately October 2022 — meaning discrete cliff/vest unlock pressure is now largely historical. The ongoing 'unlock' is a protocol-level mining emission (~882M STX remaining) distributed gradually to miners and Stackers anchored to Bitcoin block production over roughly 30 years toward a hard cap of ~1.818B STX, creating modest, predictable inflation rather than sudden unlock events. There are no known large upcoming scheduled cliff unlocks as of mid-2025.
caveats: 1) Exact per-tranche SAFT investor unlock dates and individual allocations are NOT_AVAILABLE_FREE_TIER — the full investor lockup table was in private agreements not publicly disclosed in granular form. 2) tokenunlocks.app does not appear to have a detailed live Stacks unlock schedule on its free public tier as of this research. 3) The Stacks Foundation's own treasury vesting/spending schedule post-2022 is not publicly itemized with specific unlock dates. 4) Team token figures are estimates derived from the 2019 Reg A+ offering circular (SEC filing) and Blockstack blog posts; precise monthly vest amounts per employee were not public. 5) The Nakamoto upgrade (2024) and sBTC launch may have introduced new foundation/ecosystem token programs not yet fully documented in free public sources.
